About Guizhou Normal University
Guizhou Normal University (GZNU), founded in 1941, with the former name of 芒聙聹National Guiyang Teacher芒聙聶s College芒聙聺, was one of the eight national teachers芒聙聶 colleges in China at that time. In October 1950, it was renamed as 芒聙聹Guiyang Teachers芒聙聶 College芒聙聺. It was formally named 芒聙聹Guizhou Normal University芒聙聺 in 1985 and honored as one of the provincial key universities jointly built by the Ministry of Education and the People芒聙聶s Government of Guizhou Province in 1996.
With 2 campuses, GZNU comprises 26 schools and a School of Further Education. It has more than 35,000 full-time students and a faculty team of nearly 2,600 members in total, including 327 professors and 656 associate professors. More than 80 talent and honorary titles at national level have been given to the faculty achieving great progress. GZNU has a full range of disciplines, covering 12 disciplines such as philosophy, literature, and education. The University has developed 10 doctor芒聙聶s programs in first-level disciplines, 25 master芒聙聶s programs in first-level disciplines and 25 professional ones, and more than 70 bachelor芒聙聶s programs.
Guizhou Normal University remains committed to broadening its education through global cooperation. Presently, it has established friendly partnerships and conducted programs with universities and institutions in the United States, South Korea, Japan, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, Russia and other countries, as well as Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan regions. GZNU cooperates with the Scottish Qualification Management Committee to award the UK Diploma in Higher Education program (SQA-AD), with Chinese Service Center for Scholarly Exchange (CSCSE) to offer the undergraduate international general education course (IGEC), with the National Institute of Information Technology (NIIT) of India to develop the Bachelor program of Data Science and Big Data Technology (characteristic program), and with University of Central Oklahoma on Chinese-Foreign Cooperation in Running Schools program.
Until now, more than 450,000 students have graduated from GZNU. GZNU is developing into a normal university with distinctive characteristics of teacher education and coordinated development of multi-disciplines, which has made a positive contribution to the economic and social development in Guizhou province.

Submission Guideline
All the papers received will be passed to the program chair who is in charge of the peer review process and will assign at least two suitable technical committee members as reviewers for each paper whose areas of expertise match the topics of the papers. The review comments will be sent back to the program chair who will make decisions about submissions. The conference secretary will then inform the corresponding authors about the review results. The papers which need revisions will be required to be sent back within the stipulated time, and only revised papers that pass the second review will be accepted. Please look out for emails from the conference secretary.
English is the official language of the conference.
Full paper should be at least
5 pages
and at most
10 pages
under the format.
Regular registration fee only covers one 5-page paper. Additional pages will incur extra fees.
Online Submission System
Submit to ICCBD 2026
Formatting and Publication Instructions
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Word Template
LaTeX Template
Important Dates
Submission Deadline
May 25, 2026
1. Submit your paper via EasyChair Submission System.
2. You will receive the email feedback within 2芒聙聯3 workdays to confirm whether you submit it successfully or not.
Notification of Acceptance
June 20, 2026
Authors will be notified of the review results via email, whether the paper is accepted or not. 
            Accepted papers will receive detailed instructions for registration and final submission.
Authors Registration Deadline
July 05, 2026
Accepted authors are requested to complete the registration and payment before this indicated date. 
            Please refer to the registration page for detailed instructions and required documents. 
            Late registrations may not be included in the final conference proceedings.
Listeners Registration Deadline
September 25, 2026
If you just want to attend the conference for listening without any submission, please register as listener via the link below:
https://confsys.iconf.org/register/iccbd2026
Conference Dates
September 28芒聙聯30, 2026
Detailed agenda information will be announced around September 10th.
Peer Review
The Committees of ICCBD invest great efforts in reviewing the papers submitted to the conference and organizing sessions to enable participants to gain maximum benefit. By submitting a paper to ICCBD, authors agree to the review process and understand that papers undergo a peer-review process.
Manuscripts will be reviewed by appropriately qualified experts in the field selected by the Conference Committee, who will give detailed comments and 芒聙聰 if the submission is accepted 芒聙聰 the authors submit a revised ("camera-ready") version that takes into account this feedback.
All papers are reviewed using a single-blind review process: authors declare their names and affiliations in the manuscript for reviewers to see, but reviewers do not know each other's identities, nor do the authors receive information about who has reviewed their manuscript.
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review. The most frequent cause of rejection is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed on the basis of technical quality, relevance to the conference scope, originality, significance, and clarity.
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential, and accepted papers must be revised according to referees' comments before inclusion in the proceedings.
Dual / Double Submissions
By submitting a manuscript to ICCBD, authors acknowledge that it has not been previously published or accepted for publication in substantially similar form in any peer-reviewed venue with publicly accessible papers, including journals, conferences, workshops, or other peer-reviewed archival forums. Furthermore, no paper substantially similar in content has been or will be submitted to another peer-reviewed conference or workshop during the review period. The authors also attest that they did not submit a substantially similar submission to ICCBD. As a rule of thumb, ICCBD submissions should contain no more than 30% of material from previous publications. Violation of these conditions will lead to rejection.
Plagiarism
ICCBD 2026 is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper, and scholarly citations to the work of others. Authors must clearly identify their own contributions and any published results/techniques on which they build. Reviewers ensure these standards are met.
If the author is found to commit plagiarism, the following sanctions may be applied:
Reject the article or delete it from final publications.
Report the violation to the author's supervisor(s) and affiliated institution(s).
Report the violation to the appropriate overseeing office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the author芒聙聶s name(s), article title, affiliated institution, and details of misconduct.

Call for Track Proposals
The 7th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD) invites proposals for organizing tracks. The conference aims to provide a premier platform for researchers, academics, and industry professionals to exchange innovative ideas and present cutting-edge research in the fields of computing, big data, education, Digital Image Processing, Computer Science, and related interdisciplinary areas.
We welcome experienced researchers and professionals to propose and organize tracks that focus on emerging topics, novel technologies, and practical applications. Each approved track will provide a dedicated forum for discussing specialized research themes and fostering collaboration among participants. Prospective organizers are encouraged to submit their track proposals, including the track title, scope, topics of interest, and organizer information, etc.
Benefits for Track Organizers
The track organizer will be considered as a candidate for the Conference Organization Award in recognition of their contribution to the conference.
Papers submitted under approved tracks will be eligible for a special registration discount, subject to conference policies.
If you are interested in organizing a track, please download the application form and submit the completed form to the Conference Coordinator via email at
iccbd@academic.net
.

About Invited Speaker
Serving as an Invited Speaker at an international conference offers several key benefits. 
            It provides an opportunity to present recent research achievements, enhance academic visibility, 
            and gain international recognition within the research community. The role reflects professional 
            acknowledgment of expertise and strengthens one芒聙聶s academic reputation.
Invited speakers can expand global academic networks, promote research collaboration, and exchange 
            ideas with leading scholars and industry experts. In addition, featured promotion on conference 
            platforms increases professional exposure and may lead to future speaking, editorial, or collaboration opportunities.
If you would like to participate in the conference as an Invited Speaker, please submit your biography 
            for application.

Ability to share latest academic or technological advancements

ICCBD 2023
Online
ICCBD
20
23 | 
						October 25-27, 2023 | Shanghai, China (Virtually)
2023 6th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD 2023)
Photo Gallery 
						(
Download
)
As we 
						reflect on the recently concluded 2023 6th international 
						conference on computing and big data (ICCBD 2023), held 
						from October 25th to 27th, 2023. We are thrilled to 
						share the highlights and key takeaways from this 
						enriching conference.
IICCBD 2023 witnessed a remarkable gathering of 
						researchers, professionals, and scholars from various 
						corners of the world. This diverse participation not 
						only enriched the discussions but also fostered a 
						collaborative environment that transcended geographical 
						boundaries. The conference served as a melting pot of 
						ideas, fostering interdisciplinary exchanges and 
						nurturing new avenues of research.
The conference started with welcoming remarks from Prof. 
						Weidong Li, the organizing general chair of ICCBD 2023. 
						Prof. Li's opening address was marked by warmth and 
						enthusiasm as he extended his sincere gratitude to the 
						co-organizers and extended a warm welcome to all the 
						experts and scholars in attendance. He provided insights 
						into the organization of ICCBD 2023, shedding light on 
						the meticulous planning and execution that went into 
						making the event a success. Additionally, Prof. Li 
						briefed the audience on the review process and the 
						status of article collections for the year, setting the 
						stage for the enriching discussions to follow.
Keynote 
						Speakers at ICCBD 2023
Prof. Ender Ayanoglu 
						(IEEE Fellow)
- Affiliation:University of California, USA
- Speech Title:Machine Learning in NextG Networks via 
						Generative Adversarial Networks
-Prof. Ender Ayanoglu, an esteemed IEEE Fellow from the 
						University of California, USA, captivated the audience 
						with his keynote speech on leveraging machine learning 
						techniques, particularly Generative Adversarial Networks 
						(GANs), in the context of Next Generation (NextG) 
						networks. His insightful presentation shed light on the 
						potential of GANs to revolutionize various aspects of 
						NextG networks, from optimization to security, paving 
						the way for more efficient and adaptive network 
						architectures.
Prof. LEE Kwok On Matthew
- Affiliation: City University of Hong Kong, China
- Speech Title:Why are we so addicted to cyber worlds: 
						from Tamagotchi to Metaverse
- Prof. LEE Kwok On Matthew, hailing from City 
						University of Hong Kong, China, delivered a 
						thought-provoking keynote speech on the pervasive allure 
						of virtual worlds, tracing the evolution of digital 
						engagement from the era of Tamagotchi to the burgeoning 
						Metaverse. Through engaging anecdotes and insightful 
						analysis, he explored the psychological underpinnings of 
						our fascination with cyber worlds, highlighting the 
						profound impact of technology on human behavior and 
						societal norms.
Prof. Jiangchuan (JC) Liu (IEEE Fellow)
- Affiliation:Simon Fraser University, Canada
- Speech Title:Towards Ubiquitous and Immersive 
						Networked Media: For Human and For Machine
- Prof. Jiangchuan (JC) Liu, an esteemed IEEE Fellow 
						representing Simon Fraser University, Canada, delivered 
						a visionary keynote speech on the convergence of 
						networked media and immersive technologies. His 
						presentation delved into the transformative potential of 
						ubiquitous and immersive media experiences, catering to 
						both human users and intelligent machines. Prof. Liu's 
						insights illuminated the trajectory towards a future 
						where networked media seamlessly integrates with our 
						daily lives, enriching human experiences and empowering 
						machine intelligence alike.
Invited Speakers at ICCBD 2023
1. Assoc. Prof. Wei Wang
Affiliation: Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool University, China
Speech Title: Zero-shot Text Classification with 
						External Knowledge
Assoc. Prof. Wei Wang from Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool 
						University, China, shared insights into zero-shot text 
						classification techniques augmented with external 
						knowledge. His presentation explored innovative 
						approaches to enhance text classification models, 
						leveraging external knowledge sources to improve 
						performance and adaptability in handling unseen classes.
2. Assoc. Prof. Dong Huang
Affiliation: South China Agricultural University, China
Speech Title: Recent Advances in Large-scale Graph 
						Learning
Assoc. Prof. Dong Huang, representing South China 
						Agricultural University, China, delved into the latest 
						developments in large-scale graph learning 
						methodologies. His presentation highlighted advancements 
						in algorithms and techniques tailored for analyzing and 
						extracting insights from complex network structures, 
						offering practical solutions for tackling real-world 
						challenges in various domains.
3. Prof. Petr Hajek
Affiliation: University of Pardubice, Czech Republic
Speech Title: Recent Advances in Detecting Fake Reviews 
						in E-commerce Platforms
Prof. Petr Hajek, hailing from the University of 
						Pardubice, Czech Republic, addressed the pressing issue 
						of detecting fake reviews in e-commerce platforms. His 
						presentation showcased recent advancements in the 
						development of sophisticated algorithms and strategies 
						aimed at identifying and mitigating the proliferation of 
						fraudulent reviews, safeguarding the integrity of online 
						consumer feedback systems.
4. Prof. Kin-Choong Yow
Affiliation: University of Regina, Canada
Speech Title: Advances in Generative AI (GenAI): The 
						Technology, the Applications, and the Threats
Prof. Kin-Choong Yow, representing the University of 
						Regina, Canada, provided comprehensive insights into the 
						advancements in Generative AI (GenAI). His presentation 
						covered the technological underpinnings, diverse 
						applications, and associated challenges and threats 
						posed by generative AI systems. Prof. Yow's talk offered 
						a holistic perspective on the evolving landscape of 
						AI-driven generative technologies and their implications 
						for various sectors and societal dynamics.
Moreover the conference was composed of 4 
						technical oral sessions, topics covered, for example, 
						Digital network architecture design and network security 
						monitoring, Network -based e -commerce platform 
						construction and information management, Model -based 
						visual application design and intelligent detection, 
						Data model and data analysis and so on. Each parallel session went 
						through smoothly and a 芒聙聹Best Presentation芒聙聺 award was 
						issued at the end of sub-conference.

2022 5th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD 2022)
The conference is scheduled to be held in Shanghai, China, during December 16-18, 2022. However, as coronavirus was spreading and restrictions would not allow for an on-site conference, we were prepared to run the conference in fully online mode, with virtual session rooms and facilities for online presentation of talks, so that the attendees will have an experience very close to the real conference. All the participants are requested to prepare the presentation as well and display online.

Prof. Fairouz Kamareddine
Professor of Theoretical Computer Science
Heriot-Watt University, UK
Biography
Professor Fairouz Kamareddine is a Professor of Theoretical Computer Science at Heriot-Watt University in Scotland. She has been involved in numerous worldwide consultancy assignments, particularly on education and research initiatives, working with the United Nations and the European Union.

Her research interests focus on the interface of Mathematics, Logic, and Computer Science. She has held many invited academic positions at universities across the world and has published more than 200 research articles as well as several academic books.

Professor Kamareddine has played leading roles in the development, administration, and implementation of interdisciplinary, international, and academic collaborations and research networks.

Local Visit & Travel Guide
| 忙聹卢氓聹掳氓聫聜猫搂聜忙聦聡氓聧聴
During ICCBD 2026, participants are warmly encouraged to explore Guiyang 芒聙聰 
            a city well known for its pleasant climate, rich ethnic culture, and remarkable 
            karst landscapes. The following travel recommendations introduce some of the 
            most representative attractions combining history, nature, and local culture.
Jiaxiu Tower (莽聰虏莽搂聙忙楼录)
Jiaxiu Tower, located on the Nanming River in Nanming District, is the symbolic landmark of Guiyang.
            Built during the Ming Dynasty, the tower together with Cuiwei Garden represents the historical
            and cultural identity of the city. Similar in significance to the Yellow Crane Tower in Wuhan or
            the Forbidden City in Beijing, Jiaxiu Tower reflects centuries of urban development and civilization.
            The night illumination view is especially recommended for visitors.
Address:
Jiaxiu Tower Scenic Area, Nanming District, Guiyang
Admission:
Free
Opening Hours:
Tue芒聙聯Sun 09:00芒聙聯17:00 (Closed Mondays)
Transportation:
Bus 15/34/46/48/52 to Jiaxiu Tower Station
Qingyan Ancient Town (茅聺聮氓虏漏氓聫陇茅聲聡)
Qingyan Ancient Town is one of the most famous historical towns in Guizhou Province
            and a national 5A scenic attraction. The town preserves ancient city walls, stone
            streets, temples, and traditional residences dating back hundreds of years.
            Visitors can experience authentic local architecture, traditional cuisine,
            and cultural heritage reflecting southwest China芒聙聶s historical prosperity.
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯17:00
Address:
Huaxi District, Guiyang
Ticket:
Entrance Ticket RMB 10
Transportation:
Direct tourist shuttle or Bus 203 from Guiyang Railway Station
Qianling Mountain Park (茅禄聰莽聛碌氓卤卤氓聟卢氓聸颅)
Located only 1.5 km from downtown Guiyang, Qianling Mountain Park is one of
            China芒聙聶s rare large-scale urban ecological parks. Known as the 芒聙聹First Mountain
            in Southern Guizhou,芒聙聺 it integrates mountains, forests, temples, lakes,
            wildlife habitats, and historical sites. Visitors may encounter wild macaques,
            explore Hongfu Temple, walk forest trails, or enjoy panoramic views via cableway.
Address:
Yunyan District, Guiyang
Opening Hours:
07:00芒聙聯20:00
Admission:
Free
Yelang Valley (氓陇聹茅聝聨猫掳路)
Yelang Valley is an artistic ecological park created by renowned artist Song Peilun.
            Inspired by ancient Yelang civilization and local ethnic culture, the valley combines
            stone architecture, karst landscapes, caves, forests, and artistic installations.
            Built gradually over nearly three decades, the site presents a mysterious cultural
            atmosphere blending mythology, history, and artistic imagination.
Address:
Huaxi District, Guiyang
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯16:00
Ticket:
RMB 20
Transportation:
Bus 255 or 207 to University Town
Tianhetan Scenic Area (氓陇漏忙虏鲁忙陆颅)
Tianhetan Scenic Area showcases classic karst geological landscapes featuring
            waterfalls, caves, underground rivers, natural bridges, and valleys.
            Located about 24 km from downtown Guiyang, the area integrates mountains,
            water systems, and canyon scenery, earning the reputation of a condensed
            芒聙聹landscape gallery of Guizhou.芒聙聺
Address:
Shiban Town, Huaxi District
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯18:00
Ticket:
RMB 10
Transportation:
Bus 211 or 701 to Tianhetan Resort
Guizhou Provincial Museums
The Guizhou Provincial Museum presents regional ethnic culture,
            historical relics, and natural heritage through modern exhibition
            technologies. Nearby, the Guizhou Geological Museum introduces
            geological evolution, fossil discoveries, and dinosaur exhibitions,
            providing an educational experience for visitors interested in
            earth science and regional history.
Location:
Guanshanhu District, Guiyang
Admission:
Free (Reservation Required)
Highlights:
Ethnic Culture 芒聙垄 Fossils 芒聙垄 Geological Exhibitions

Welcome to ICCBD 2026
2026 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing and Big Data
September 28-30, 2026 | Guiyang,Guizhou Province, China
2026 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD 2026)
will be held on
September 28芒聙聯30, 2026
, 
    in
Guiyang, China
. 
    The conference is sponsored by
Guizhou Normal University,
and hosted by the
School of Big Data and Computer Science, Guizhou Normal University
.
ICCBD 2026
, themed
芒聙聹Data-Centric and AI-Driven Computing for Real-World Intelligence芒聙聺
, 
    aims to provide a
high-level international forum
for researchers and practitioners to present and discuss recent advances in
computing and big data
. 

    The conference addresses emerging topics related to
big data analytics
,
artificial intelligence
,
cloud and edge computing
, 
    and
intelligent data-driven systems
, 
    with an emphasis on both
theoretical foundations
and
practical applications
. 

    ICCBD 2026 seeks to promote
academic exchange
, 
    foster
international collaboration
, 
    and support the development of
innovative technologies
in computing and big data.

Submission
We welcome Full Papers (for publication) and Abstracts (for presentation only). Manuscripts must be in English.

Academic Ethics
Academic integrity represents the
ethical standards and moral code
of the scholarly community. It includes principles such as the
avoidance of cheating or plagiarism
, maintaining
rigorous academic standards
, and
honesty in research and scholarly publishing
.
All authors are expected to comply with these principles. Failure to do so may result in the committee rejecting the manuscript during the review process. Due to ongoing updates to academic databases and the lengthy publication process, if the publisher identifies instances of plagiarism or a high degree of content similarity in a submitted paper, the manuscript may be directly rejected. Authors bear full responsibility for any violations of these standards.
Call for Papers
Innovative research contributions are solicited on the following topics:
Topic 1: Data-Centric Computing Architectures and Platforms
Topic 2: Big Data Analytics, Models and Algorithms
Topic 3: Visualization, Image Processing and Multimedia Analytics
Topic 4: Blockchain Technologies and Distributed Intelligent Systems
Topic 5: Networking, IoT and Cybersecurity for Big Data Systems
Topic 6: Intelligent Energy Systems and Smart Infrastructure Analytics
Topic 7: Big Data Science, Management and Governance
Topic 8: Big Data and Intelligent Computing for Education and Society

Academic Ethics
Academic integrity represents the
ethical standards and moral code
of the scholarly community. It includes principles such as the
avoidance of cheating or plagiarism
, maintaining
rigorous academic standards
, and
honesty in research and scholarly publishing
.
All authors are expected to comply with these principles. Failure to do so may result in the committee rejecting the manuscript during the review process. Due to ongoing updates to academic databases and the lengthy publication process, if the publisher identifies instances of plagiarism or a high degree of content similarity in a submitted paper, the manuscript may be directly rejected. Authors bear full responsibility for any violations of these standards.
Call for Papers
Innovative research contributions are solicited on the following topics:
Topic 1: Data-Centric Computing Architectures and Platforms
Topic 2: Big Data Analytics, Models and Algorithms
Topic 3: Visualization, Image Processing and Multimedia Analytics
Topic 4: Blockchain Technologies and Distributed Intelligent Systems
Topic 5: Networking, IoT and Cybersecurity for Big Data Systems
Topic 6: Intelligent Energy Systems and Smart Infrastructure Analytics
Topic 7: Big Data Science, Management and Governance
Topic 8: Big Data and Intelligent Computing for Education and Society

Keynote Speakers茂录聢to be updated)
| 氓陇搂盲录職盲赂禄猫庐虏氓聵聣氓庐戮(氓戮聟忙聸麓忙聳掳)
Prof. Cheng-Zhong Xu
IEEE Fellow
University of Macau
Biography
Dr. Cheng-Zhong Xu, IEEE Fellow, is the Dean of Faculty of Science and Technology and the Interim Director of Institute of Collaborative Innovation, University of Macau, and a Chair Professor of Computer and Information Science. He was a professor of Wayne State University and the Director of Institute of Advanced Computing of Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences before joining UM in 2019.

Dr. Xu is a Chief Scientist of the Key Project on Smart City of MOST, China and a Principal Investigator of the Key Project on Autonomous Driving of FDCT, Macau SAR. His research interests focus on parallel and distributed computing and cloud computing, particularly resource management for performance, reliability, availability, power efficiency, and security, as well as data-driven intelligence applications in smart cities and autonomous driving systems.

He has published two research monographs and more than 300 peer-reviewed papers and received multiple Best Paper Awards and nominations from leading IEEE and ACM conferences. He also holds more than 100 patents and founded Shenzhen Baidou Applied Technology dedicated to location-based services.

Dr. Xu received the prestigious President芒聙聶s Awards for Excellence in Teaching at Wayne State University. He has served on editorial boards of leading journals including IEEE Transactions on Computers, IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing, IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, and Journal of Parallel and Distributed Computing. Since 2015, he has served as Chair of the IEEE Technical Committee on Distributed Processing.

| 莽聣鹿茅聜聙忙聤楼氓聭聤氓聵聣氓庐戮(氓戮聟忙聸麓忙聳掳)
Prof. Fairouz Kamareddine
Professor of Theoretical Computer Science
Heriot-Watt University, UK
Biography
Professor Fairouz Kamareddine is a Professor of Theoretical Computer Science at Heriot-Watt University in Scotland. She has been involved in numerous worldwide consultancy assignments, particularly on education and research initiatives, working with the United Nations and the European Union.

Her research interests focus on the interface of Mathematics, Logic, and Computer Science. She has held many invited academic positions at universities across the world and has published more than 200 research articles as well as several academic books.

Professor Kamareddine has played leading roles in the development, administration, and implementation of interdisciplinary, international, and academic collaborations and research networks.

About Guizhou Normal University
Guizhou Normal University (GZNU), founded in 1941, with the former name of 芒聙聹National Guiyang Teacher芒聙聶s College芒聙聺, was one of the eight national teachers芒聙聶 colleges in China at that time. In October 1950, it was renamed as 芒聙聹Guiyang Teachers芒聙聶 College芒聙聺. It was formally named 芒聙聹Guizhou Normal University芒聙聺 in 1985 and honored as one of the provincial key universities jointly built by the Ministry of Education and the People芒聙聶s Government of Guizhou Province in 1996.
With 2 campuses, GZNU comprises 26 schools and a School of Further Education. It has more than 35,000 full-time students and a faculty team of nearly 2,600 members in total, including 327 professors and 656 associate professors. More than 80 talent and honorary titles at national level have been given to the faculty achieving great progress. GZNU has a full range of disciplines, covering 12 disciplines such as philosophy, literature, and education. The University has developed 10 doctor芒聙聶s programs in first-level disciplines, 25 master芒聙聶s programs in first-level disciplines and 25 professional ones, and more than 70 bachelor芒聙聶s programs.
Guizhou Normal University remains committed to broadening its education through global cooperation. Presently, it has established friendly partnerships and conducted programs with universities and institutions in the United States, South Korea, Japan, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, Russia and other countries, as well as Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan regions. GZNU cooperates with the Scottish Qualification Management Committee to award the UK Diploma in Higher Education program (SQA-AD), with Chinese Service Center for Scholarly Exchange (CSCSE) to offer the undergraduate international general education course (IGEC), with the National Institute of Information Technology (NIIT) of India to develop the Bachelor program of Data Science and Big Data Technology (characteristic program), and with University of Central Oklahoma on Chinese-Foreign Cooperation in Running Schools program.
Until now, more than 450,000 students have graduated from GZNU. GZNU is developing into a normal university with distinctive characteristics of teacher education and coordinated development of multi-disciplines, which has made a positive contribution to the economic and social development in Guizhou province.

Local Visit & Travel Guide
| 忙聹卢氓聹掳氓聫聜猫搂聜忙聦聡氓聧聴
During ICCBD 2026, participants are warmly encouraged to explore Guiyang 芒聙聰 
            a city well known for its pleasant climate, rich ethnic culture, and remarkable 
            karst landscapes. The following travel recommendations introduce some of the 
            most representative attractions combining history, nature, and local culture.
Jiaxiu Tower (莽聰虏莽搂聙忙楼录)
Jiaxiu Tower, located on the Nanming River in Nanming District, is the symbolic landmark of Guiyang.
            Built during the Ming Dynasty, the tower together with Cuiwei Garden represents the historical
            and cultural identity of the city. Similar in significance to the Yellow Crane Tower in Wuhan or
            the Forbidden City in Beijing, Jiaxiu Tower reflects centuries of urban development and civilization.
            The night illumination view is especially recommended for visitors.
Address:
Jiaxiu Tower Scenic Area, Nanming District, Guiyang
Admission:
Free
Opening Hours:
Tue芒聙聯Sun 09:00芒聙聯17:00 (Closed Mondays)
Transportation:
Bus 15/34/46/48/52 to Jiaxiu Tower Station
Qingyan Ancient Town (茅聺聮氓虏漏氓聫陇茅聲聡)
Qingyan Ancient Town is one of the most famous historical towns in Guizhou Province
            and a national 5A scenic attraction. The town preserves ancient city walls, stone
            streets, temples, and traditional residences dating back hundreds of years.
            Visitors can experience authentic local architecture, traditional cuisine,
            and cultural heritage reflecting southwest China芒聙聶s historical prosperity.
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯17:00
Address:
Huaxi District, Guiyang
Ticket:
Entrance Ticket RMB 10
Transportation:
Direct tourist shuttle or Bus 203 from Guiyang Railway Station
Qianling Mountain Park (茅禄聰莽聛碌氓卤卤氓聟卢氓聸颅)
Located only 1.5 km from downtown Guiyang, Qianling Mountain Park is one of
            China芒聙聶s rare large-scale urban ecological parks. Known as the 芒聙聹First Mountain
            in Southern Guizhou,芒聙聺 it integrates mountains, forests, temples, lakes,
            wildlife habitats, and historical sites. Visitors may encounter wild macaques,
            explore Hongfu Temple, walk forest trails, or enjoy panoramic views via cableway.
Address:
Yunyan District, Guiyang
Opening Hours:
07:00芒聙聯20:00
Admission:
Free
Yelang Valley (氓陇聹茅聝聨猫掳路)
Yelang Valley is an artistic ecological park created by renowned artist Song Peilun.
            Inspired by ancient Yelang civilization and local ethnic culture, the valley combines
            stone architecture, karst landscapes, caves, forests, and artistic installations.
            Built gradually over nearly three decades, the site presents a mysterious cultural
            atmosphere blending mythology, history, and artistic imagination.
Address:
Huaxi District, Guiyang
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯16:00
Ticket:
RMB 20
Transportation:
Bus 255 or 207 to University Town
Tianhetan Scenic Area (氓陇漏忙虏鲁忙陆颅)
Tianhetan Scenic Area showcases classic karst geological landscapes featuring
            waterfalls, caves, underground rivers, natural bridges, and valleys.
            Located about 24 km from downtown Guiyang, the area integrates mountains,
            water systems, and canyon scenery, earning the reputation of a condensed
            芒聙聹landscape gallery of Guizhou.芒聙聺
Address:
Shiban Town, Huaxi District
Opening Hours:
08:30芒聙聯18:00
Ticket:
RMB 10

Bus 211 or 701 to Tianhetan Resort
Guizhou Provincial Museums
The Guizhou Provincial Museum presents regional ethnic culture,
            historical relics, and natural heritage through modern exhibition
            technologies. Nearby, the Guizhou Geological Museum introduces
            geological evolution, fossil discoveries, and dinosaur exhibitions,
            providing an educational experience for visitors interested in
            earth science and regional history.

Guanshanhu District, Guiyang

Free (Reservation Required)

Conference participants may need a Chinese visa to enter China.

.

.

Submission Instruction
| 忙聤聲莽篓驴猫炉娄忙聝
Submission Guideline
All the papers received will be passed to the program chair who is in charge of the peer review process and will assign at least two suitable technical committee members as reviewers for each paper whose areas of expertise match the topics of the papers. The review comments will be sent back to the program chair who will make decisions about submissions. The conference secretary will then inform the corresponding authors about the review results. The papers which need revisions will be required to be sent back within the stipulated time, and only revised papers that pass the second review will be accepted. Please look out for emails from the conference secretary.
English is the official language of the conference.
Full paper should be at least
5 pages
and at most
10 pages
under the format.
Regular registration fee only covers one 5-page paper. Additional pages will incur extra fees.
Online Submission System
Submit to ICCBD 2026
Formatting and Publication Instructions
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Word Template
LaTeX Template
Important Dates
Submission Deadline
May 25, 2026
1. Submit your paper via EasyChair Submission System.
2. You will receive the email feedback within 2芒聙聯3 workdays to confirm whether you submit it successfully or not.
Notification of Acceptance
June 20, 2026
Authors will be notified of the review results via email, whether the paper is accepted or not. 
            Accepted papers will receive detailed instructions for registration and final submission.
Authors Registration Deadline
July 05, 2026
Accepted authors are requested to complete the registration and payment before this indicated date. 
            Please refer to the registration page for detailed instructions and required documents. 
            Late registrations may not be included in the final conference proceedings.
Listeners Registration Deadline
September 25, 2026
If you just want to attend the conference for listening without any submission, please register as listener via the link below:
https://confsys.iconf.org/register/iccbd2026
Conference Dates
September 28芒聙聯30, 2026
Detailed agenda information will be announced around September 10th.
Peer Review
The Committees of ICCBD invest great efforts in reviewing the papers submitted to the conference and organizing sessions to enable participants to gain maximum benefit. By submitting a paper to ICCBD, authors agree to the review process and understand that papers undergo a peer-review process.
Manuscripts will be reviewed by appropriately qualified experts in the field selected by the Conference Committee, who will give detailed comments and 芒聙聰 if the submission is accepted 芒聙聰 the authors submit a revised ("camera-ready") version that takes into account this feedback.
All papers are reviewed using a single-blind review process: authors declare their names and affiliations in the manuscript for reviewers to see, but reviewers do not know each other's identities, nor do the authors receive information about who has reviewed their manuscript.
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review. The most frequent cause of rejection is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed on the basis of technical quality, relevance to the conference scope, originality, significance, and clarity.
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential, and accepted papers must be revised according to referees' comments before inclusion in the proceedings.
Dual / Double Submissions
By submitting a manuscript to ICCBD, authors acknowledge that it has not been previously published or accepted for publication in substantially similar form in any peer-reviewed venue with publicly accessible papers, including journals, conferences, workshops, or other peer-reviewed archival forums. Furthermore, no paper substantially similar in content has been or will be submitted to another peer-reviewed conference or workshop during the review period. The authors also attest that they did not submit a substantially similar submission to ICCBD. As a rule of thumb, ICCBD submissions should contain no more than 30% of material from previous publications. Violation of these conditions will lead to rejection.
Plagiarism
ICCBD 2026 is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper, and scholarly citations to the work of others. Authors must clearly identify their own contributions and any published results/techniques on which they build. Reviewers ensure these standards are met.
If the author is found to commit plagiarism, the following sanctions may be applied:
Reject the article or delete it from final publications.
Report the violation to the author's supervisor(s) and affiliated institution(s).
Report the violation to the appropriate overseeing office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the author芒聙聶s name(s), article title, affiliated institution, and details of misconduct.

Call for Track Proposals
The 7th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD) invites proposals for organizing tracks. The conference aims to provide a premier platform for researchers, academics, and industry professionals to exchange innovative ideas and present cutting-edge research in the fields of computing, big data, education, Digital Image Processing, Computer Science, and related interdisciplinary areas.
We welcome experienced researchers and professionals to propose and organize tracks that focus on emerging topics, novel technologies, and practical applications. Each approved track will provide a dedicated forum for discussing specialized research themes and fostering collaboration among participants. Prospective organizers are encouraged to submit their track proposals, including the track title, scope, topics of interest, and organizer information, etc.
Benefits for Track Organizers
The track organizer will be considered as a candidate for the Conference Organization Award in recognition of their contribution to the conference.
Papers submitted under approved tracks will be eligible for a special registration discount, subject to conference policies.
If you are interested in organizing a track, please download the application form and submit the completed form to the Conference Coordinator via email at
iccbd@academic.net
.

            for application.

As we 
						reflect on the recently concluded 2023 6th international 
						conference on computing and big data (ICCBD 2023), held 
						from October 25th to 27th, 2023. We are thrilled to 
						share the highlights and key takeaways from this 
						enriching conference.
IICCBD 2023 witnessed a remarkable gathering of 
						researchers, professionals, and scholars from various 
						corners of the world. This diverse participation not 
						only enriched the discussions but also fostered a 
						collaborative environment that transcended geographical 
						boundaries. The conference served as a melting pot of 
						ideas, fostering interdisciplinary exchanges and 
						nurturing new avenues of research.
The conference started with welcoming remarks from Prof. 
						Weidong Li, the organizing general chair of ICCBD 2023. 
						Prof. Li's opening address was marked by warmth and 
						enthusiasm as he extended his sincere gratitude to the 
						co-organizers and extended a warm welcome to all the 
						experts and scholars in attendance. He provided insights 
						into the organization of ICCBD 2023, shedding light on 
						the meticulous planning and execution that went into 
						making the event a success. Additionally, Prof. Li 
						briefed the audience on the review process and the 
						status of article collections for the year, setting the 
						stage for the enriching discussions to follow.
Keynote 
						Speakers at ICCBD 2023
Prof. Ender Ayanoglu 
						(IEEE Fellow)
- Affiliation:University of California, USA
- Speech Title:Machine Learning in NextG Networks via 
						Generative Adversarial Networks
-Prof. Ender Ayanoglu, an esteemed IEEE Fellow from the 
						University of California, USA, captivated the audience 
						with his keynote speech on leveraging machine learning 
						techniques, particularly Generative Adversarial Networks 
						(GANs), in the context of Next Generation (NextG) 
						networks. His insightful presentation shed light on the 
						potential of GANs to revolutionize various aspects of 
						NextG networks, from optimization to security, paving 
						the way for more efficient and adaptive network 
						architectures.
Prof. LEE Kwok On Matthew
- Affiliation: City University of Hong Kong, China
- Speech Title:Why are we so addicted to cyber worlds: 
						from Tamagotchi to Metaverse
- Prof. LEE Kwok On Matthew, hailing from City 
						University of Hong Kong, China, delivered a 
						thought-provoking keynote speech on the pervasive allure 
						of virtual worlds, tracing the evolution of digital 
						engagement from the era of Tamagotchi to the burgeoning 
						Metaverse. Through engaging anecdotes and insightful 
						analysis, he explored the psychological underpinnings of 
						our fascination with cyber worlds, highlighting the 
						profound impact of technology on human behavior and 
						societal norms.
Prof. Jiangchuan (JC) Liu (IEEE Fellow)
- Affiliation:Simon Fraser University, Canada
- Speech Title:Towards Ubiquitous and Immersive 
						Networked Media: For Human and For Machine
- Prof. Jiangchuan (JC) Liu, an esteemed IEEE Fellow 
						representing Simon Fraser University, Canada, delivered 
						a visionary keynote speech on the convergence of 
						networked media and immersive technologies. His 
						presentation delved into the transformative potential of 
						ubiquitous and immersive media experiences, catering to 
						both human users and intelligent machines. Prof. Liu's 
						insights illuminated the trajectory towards a future 
						where networked media seamlessly integrates with our 
						daily lives, enriching human experiences and empowering 
						machine intelligence alike.
Invited Speakers at ICCBD 2023
1. Assoc. Prof. Wei Wang
Affiliation: Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool University, China
Speech Title: Zero-shot Text Classification with 
						External Knowledge
Assoc. Prof. Wei Wang from Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool 
						University, China, shared insights into zero-shot text 
						classification techniques augmented with external 
						knowledge. His presentation explored innovative 
						approaches to enhance text classification models, 
						leveraging external knowledge sources to improve 
						performance and adaptability in handling unseen classes.
2. Assoc. Prof. Dong Huang
Affiliation: South China Agricultural University, China
Speech Title: Recent Advances in Large-scale Graph 
						Learning
Assoc. Prof. Dong Huang, representing South China 
						Agricultural University, China, delved into the latest 
						developments in large-scale graph learning 
						methodologies. His presentation highlighted advancements 
						in algorithms and techniques tailored for analyzing and 
						extracting insights from complex network structures, 
						offering practical solutions for tackling real-world 
						challenges in various domains.
3. Prof. Petr Hajek
Affiliation: University of Pardubice, Czech Republic
Speech Title: Recent Advances in Detecting Fake Reviews 
						in E-commerce Platforms
Prof. Petr Hajek, hailing from the University of 
						Pardubice, Czech Republic, addressed the pressing issue 
						of detecting fake reviews in e-commerce platforms. His 
						presentation showcased recent advancements in the 
						development of sophisticated algorithms and strategies 
						aimed at identifying and mitigating the proliferation of 
						fraudulent reviews, safeguarding the integrity of online 
						consumer feedback systems.
4. Prof. Kin-Choong Yow
Affiliation: University of Regina, Canada
Speech Title: Advances in Generative AI (GenAI): The 
						Technology, the Applications, and the Threats
Prof. Kin-Choong Yow, representing the University of 
						Regina, Canada, provided comprehensive insights into the 
						advancements in Generative AI (GenAI). His presentation 
						covered the technological underpinnings, diverse 
						applications, and associated challenges and threats 
						posed by generative AI systems. Prof. Yow's talk offered 
						a holistic perspective on the evolving landscape of 
						AI-driven generative technologies and their implications 
						for various sectors and societal dynamics.
Moreover the conference was composed of 4 
						technical oral sessions, topics covered, for example, 
						Digital network architecture design and network security 
						monitoring, Network -based e -commerce platform 
						construction and information management, Model -based 
						visual application design and intelligent detection, 
						Data model and data analysis and so on. Each parallel session went 
						through smoothly and a 芒聙聹Best Presentation芒聙聺 award was 
						issued at the end of sub-conference.

2022 5th International Conference on Computing and Big Data (ICCBD 2022)
The conference is scheduled to be held in Shanghai, China, during December 16-18, 2022. However, as coronavirus was spreading and restrictions would not allow for an on-site conference, we were prepared to run the conference in fully online mode, with virtual session rooms and facilities for online presentation of talks, so that the attendees will have an experience very close to the real conference. All the participants are requested to prepare the presentation as well and display online.
